open_webhook_url_attacker_controlled.description: Endpoint accepts attacker-supplied webhook URL and the server POSTs to it -- classic SSRF/exfil vector, often hidden behind 'integrations' / 'notifications'

probes.description: Set webhook to Collaborator -- if interaction received with server's IP, outbound is attacker-controlled (SSRF + data-exfil through partner-callback shape)

third_party_response_trust_no_validation.description: Server fetches from third-party API and reflects response into the user's session without sanitization (geocoding, image proxy, link unfurl, social embed) -- attacker controls the upstream content via spoofed redirect target

probes.description: Point image/link unfurl at attacker host -- if Collaborator sees the fetch, server makes outbound. Then inspect target response for whether attacker content reflects back (SSRF chain to stored XSS / SSRF to internal asset).

oauth_callback_origin_trust.description: OAuth callback handler trusts `state` / `next_url` / `return_to` without origin check -- attacker-supplied URL receives the auth code or session token

probes.description: Supply external URL in next/return param -- 302 with external Location = OAuth-callback redirect open. Pair with PKCE-reuse or state-reuse for ATO chain.

partner_redirect_chain_ssrf.description: Server uses partner-API URL that redirects back into internal infra (CDN, asset-resolver, link-shortener) -- attacker uses partner's open-redirect to reach internal targets the WAF didn't expect

probes.description: Use known partner shortener that follows redirects -- if server follows the chain, it reaches cloud metadata. Try common shorteners: bit.ly, t.co, tinyurl, goo.gl, ow.ly.

saml_idp_trust_no_signature_check.description: SAML responder accepts unsigned assertion or trusts attacker-controlled IdP metadata URL -- supply attacker IdP, attacker forges assertion for any user

probes.description: Provide attacker metadata URL -- if Collaborator sees a fetch, SP downloads attacker IdP metadata = SAML federation hijack

rss_atom_feed_xxe.description: Server consumes user-supplied feed URL (RSS reader, podcast importer, OPML import) -- attacker hosts XML with XXE/SSRF/billion-laughs payload

probes.description: Attacker hosts XML referencing external entity -- if server fetches AND parses XML libraries with external-entities enabled, XXE chain available

webhook_signature_trust_no_origin_check.description: Incoming webhook (Stripe, Twilio, GitHub, Slack) accepted from any IP -- attacker forges webhook body if HMAC secret leaks OR if signature header is unchecked

probes.description: Replay webhook body with the Stripe-Signature / X-Hub-Signature header removed -- 200 = signature unchecked, anyone can forge webhook events

json_response_field_injection_via_upstre.description: Server returns upstream JSON wrapped without escaping -- if attacker controls upstream (DNS poison, link-unfurl, social-embed), they inject keys that override caller's auth context downstream

probes.description: Host upstream JSON with extra keys like `is_admin:true`, `role:"admin"`, `user_id:1` -- if server reflects upstream JSON verbatim into its own response, downstream consumers may trust forged fields
